prompt: Pharmaceutical vial product photography, vertical 4:5 portrait composition. The reference image shows the locked vial geometry, navy blue cap (hex #101850), studio lighting, label material, and bottom navy accent bar — reproduce all of these identically. Cap color must be EXACTLY hex #101850, do not drift lighter or darker. The ONLY changes versus the reference: the label gains compliance and dose text below the product name. NEW LABEL CONTENT — top to bottom: (1) Upper roughly 60 percent of the label is a blank pristine white logo zone with absolutely no graphics, text, lines, or marks of any kind. (2) Below the logo zone, centered, the product name 'BPC-157' in clean modern medium-bold geometric medical sans-serif typography in dark charcoal grey, natural letter proportions (NOT vertically stretched, NOT condensed). (3) Directly below the product name with tight spacing, centered, the dose-and-volume line '5 mg · 3 ml' in the same medical sans-serif typeface but at roughly 60 percent of the product-name size, charcoal grey, with a single middle-dot separator between the dose and the vial volume. (4) Below the dose with a small visual gap, centered, in tiny clean all-caps medical sans-serif at roughly 25 percent of the product-name size, in medium grey, the compliance line: 'FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY · NOT FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION'. (5) Directly below the compliance line, centered, in the same tiny medium grey type, the lot line: 'LOT: NH-1024'. (6) At the very bottom edge of the label, the slim solid navy blue accent bar at exactly hex #101850 running edge to edge — the only horizontal line on the label. Absolutely NO hairlines anywhere, NO horizontal rules above or below the text elements, just the single solid bar at the bottom. All four text elements are perfectly horizontally centered and stacked tight in the lower portion of the label. Sharp focus, high resolution, premium pharmaceutical product shot, clinical minimalist aesthetic.
